title: More Developers =/= Faster? Why Teams DON'T Scale! #shorts
summary: Adding more developers to engineering teams often reduces productivity due to communication overhead and coordination complexity. Effective scaling requires thoughtful team structure rather than simply increasing headcount.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that adding developers to an engineering team often lowers productivity rather than raising it, because communication overhead and coordination complexity grow as headcount increases. The takeaway is that effective scaling depends on thoughtful team structure, not on simply hiring more people.
